ip_outcome_note: Card name and Mushu flavor text confirm Cri-Kee from Disney's Mulan (1998). Disney Wiki documents the character as a lucky cricket gifted to Mulan by Grandmother Fa, created by Disney veteran Joe Grant. External Disney IP — IP warning block added.

trivia_bullets.text: Cri-Kee is the lucky cricket companion in Disney's Mulan (1998), purchased by Grandmother Fa as a luck charm. He secretly confesses to Mushu that he is not actually lucky, though he survives every peril in the film. Created by Disney veteran Joe Grant (on staff since Snow White, 1937).

Reign of Jafar is Disney Lorcana Set 8, released May 30, 2025 (LGS) and June 6, 2025 (retail), spanning multiple Disney franchises. Despite the Aladdin-themed name, Mulan characters including Cri-Kee appear here as part of a Mulan cohort; the Mulan/Lilo & Stitch Ruby/Steel starter deck confirms Mulan as a featured franchise.
sources: LorcanaPlayer.com: Reign of Jafar card list

trivia_bullets.text: "You were so focused on me and Mulan that you forgot about my man Cri-Kee here. And that was a mistake!" — Mushu. Speaker is Mushu, the disgraced dragon ancestor spirit and Mulan's guardian (voiced by Eddie Murphy, 1998). The quote frames Cri-Kee as a hidden threat underestimated due to size.
sources: Lorcana card database; frontmatter flavor_text field

trivia_bullets.text: 4-cost Ruby Character (Storyborn Ally, Common), 4 Strength / 3 Willpower / 1 Lore. AT HER SIDE grants +2 Strength while 2 or more other exerted characters are in play, making him a 6-Strength threat on an active wide board.
sources: Mushu Report: Cri-Kee — Part of the Team
